pyeto.description: Calculate duration based on the FAO56 calculation from the PyETO library

static.description: 'Dummy' module with a static configurable delta

passthrough.description: Passthrough module that returns the value of an Evapotranspiration sensor as delta

automatic-duration-calculation.description: Calculation takes collected weather data up to that point and updates the bucket for each automatic zone. Then, the duration is adjusted based on the new bucket value and the collected weather data is removed.

errors.warning-update-time-on-or-after-calc-tim: Warning: weather data update time on or after calculation time

automatic-update.description: Collect and store weather data automatically. Weather data is required to calculate zone buckets and durations.

automatic-clear.description: Automatically remove collected weather data at a configured time. Use this to make sure that there is no left over weather data from previous days. Don't remove the weather data before you calculate and only use this option if you expect the automatic update to collect weather data after you calculated for the day. Ideally, you want to prune as late in the day as possible.

continuousupdates.description: This experimental feature will continuously update the sensor data. This is useful for sensor groups that use sources that provide continuous data, such as weather stations. This feature cannot be used for sensor groups that at least partly rely on weather services as continous polling of APIs will incur costs. Keep in mind that this is experimental and may not work as expected. Use at your own risk.

schedules.title: Schedules
schedules.description: Create recurring schedules to automatically irrigate your zones at specific times. No automations needed.
schedules.no_items: No schedules configured yet. Click 'Add Schedule' to get started.

fields.name: Name
fields.account_for_duration: Start early so irrigation finishes at trigger time

weather_data.forecast_none: Forecast is available when a weather service is enabled.

advanced.used_by_zones: Used by {count, plural, one {# zone} other {# zones}}

experimental.title: Experimental features
experimental.warning: These features are opt-in and still being refined. They change how each zone's bucket is filled, so turn them on one at a time and keep an eye on your zones — you can switch them back off at any time.

forecast_weighting.title: Forecast-weighted durations
forecast_weighting.description: Instead of skipping a whole run when rain is forecast, water less. The upcoming precipitation (over the look-ahead window set under When to Water) is subtracted from the deficit used to compute the run duration, while the true deficit stays in the bucket so the real rain fills the rest. If the forecast rain misses, the next run makes up the difference. Requires a weather service.
forecast_weighting.label: Reduce durations when rain is forecast
forecast_weighting.note: Uses the precipitation look-ahead from When to Water. Works alongside the rain-skip guard (a skip still wins over a reduced run).

observed_watering.title: Credit bucket from observed watering
observed_watering.description: When a zone's linked valve runs outside Smart Irrigation — a manual tap, an automation, your own schedule — its bucket is credited for the water applied, estimated from the run time and the zone's throughput. This keeps the soil-moisture model honest when you water by other means. Smart Irrigation's own runs are already accounted for and are never double-counted.
observed_watering.label: Credit the bucket when a linked valve runs externally
observed_watering.note: Requires a linked valve and a throughput on the zone. Volume is estimated (run time × throughput), not metered.

how-to-get-help.title: How to get help
how-to-get-help.if-you-still-need-help: If you still need help reach out on the

info.title: Info
info.description: View information about next irrigation and system status.

irrigate_now.title: Irrigate Now
irrigate_now.description: Immediately start irrigation for all zones that have a linked entity. Skip conditions are ignored.
irrigate_now.no_linked_zones: No zones have a linked switch/valve entity with a calculated duration.

errors.cannot-delete-mapping-because-zones-use-: You cannot delete this sensor group because there is at least one zone using it.
errors.source_does_not_exist: Source does not exist. Please enter a valid source, such as 'sensor.mysensor'.

mapping.sensor-aggregate-of-sensor-values-to-cal: of sensor values to calculate duration

mappings.description: Add one or more sensor groups that retrieve weather data from Weather service, from sensors or a combination of these. You can map each sensor group to one or more zones
mappings.no_items: There are no sensor group defined yet.
mappings.title: Sensor Groups

weather-records.title: Weather Records
weather-records.no-data: No weather data available for this sensor group

errors.cannot-delete-module-because-zones-use-i: You cannot delete this module because there is at least one zone using it.

translated-options.EstimateFromSunHoursAndTemperature: Estimate from average of sun hours and temperature

coastal.name: Coastal
coastal.description: Enable if the weather station is located near a coast or large body of water. Affects how atmospheric humidity is estimated.

solrad_behavior.name: Solar radiation estimation
solrad_behavior.description: How solar radiation is estimated when it is not directly measured by a sensor.

forecast_days.name: Forecast days
forecast_days.description: Number of future days to include in the ET calculation. 0 = current weather only (recommended — no extra API calls). Values > 0 average today's ET with forecasted ET for upcoming days (up to 4 days via the OWM free tier).

delta.name: Delta
delta.description: Static evapotranspiration delta (mm) used directly without any weather-based calculation.

modules.description: Add one or more modules that calculate irrigation duration. Each module comes with its own configuration and can be used to calculate duration for one or more zones.
modules.no_items: There are no modules defined yet.
modules.title: Modules

zones.description: Specify one or more irrigation zones here. The irrigation duration is calculated per zone, depending on size, throughput, state, module and sensor group.
zones.no_items: There are no zones defined yet.
zones.title: Zones

status.decision_disabled: Turned off — this zone won't be watered automatically.
status.decision_water: Watering needed: about {duration} on the next scheduled run.
status.decision_water_skip: Deficit ~{duration}, but the next run will likely be skipped ({reason}).
status.decision_water_no_schedule: Deficit ~{duration} — no schedule waters this zone; trigger it manually.
status.decision_no_water: No watering needed right now — the soil has enough moisture.
status.decision_unknown: Not calculated yet — press Update, then Calculate to check.

estimate_method.hourly: Live estimate from hourly weather since the last calculation
estimate_method.proxy: Estimate distributed from today's forecast since the last calculation

help.bucket: Soil-moisture balance. A negative value means the soil is dry and the zone needs water.
help.calculate: Works out how long to water from the latest data. Run this after Update.
help.update: Fetches the latest weather/sensor data for this zone.
help.irrigate_link_entity: Link a switch/valve in this zone's settings to enable manual watering.
help.irrigate_all: Opens the linked valves now for every zone with a deficit. Skip conditions (rain, wind, temperature) are ignored.
help.update_all: Collects the latest weather/sensor data for all zones. Does not change durations on its own.
help.calculate_all: Recomputes each automatic zone's watering duration from the data collected so far.

outlook.no_schedule: No automatic schedule — zones water only when you trigger them.
outlook.will_skip: Next run will likely be skipped
outlook.will_run: Conditions look clear for the next run.

calendar.no_data: No watering calendar data available for this zone.

confirm_action.reset_bucket_body: This sets the bucket back to 0, discarding the accumulated moisture balance for this zone.
confirm_action.reset_all_buckets_body: This sets every zone's bucket back to 0, discarding the accumulated moisture balance. Watering calculations start fresh from the next update.
confirm_action.clear_weather_body: This deletes all collected weather and sensor records for every zone. Zones will need fresh data before they can calculate again.

confirm_irrigate.title: Start irrigation?
confirm_irrigate.body: This opens the linked valve(s) now and bypasses all skip conditions (rain, temperature, minimum days between watering).

weather_service_config.title: Weather Service
weather_service_config.description: Configure which weather service to use for ET calculations and skip conditions.
weather_service_config.api_key_placeholder: Leave blank to keep existing key
weather_service_config.api_key_help: An API key from your chosen weather service provider. Open-Meteo does not require a key. OpenWeatherMap and Pirate Weather both offer free tiers.
weather_service_config.no_api_key_needed: Open-Meteo is a free service and requires no API key.
weather_service_config.test_error_invalid_auth: ✗ Invalid API key — check that it is correct and active
weather_service_config.test_error_cannot_connect: ✗ Cannot connect — check your internet connection

irrigation_start_triggers.title: Irrigation Start Triggers
irrigation_start_triggers.description: Configure when irrigation should start based on solar events. You can add multiple triggers for different schedules. For sunrise triggers, leaving offset at 0 will automatically use the total duration of all enabled zones.

name.name: Trigger Name
name.description: A descriptive name to identify this trigger

type.name: Trigger Type
type.description: The type of solar event to trigger on

enabled.name: Enabled
enabled.description: Whether this trigger is currently active

offset_minutes.name: Offset (minutes)
offset_minutes.description: Minutes before (-) or after (+) the solar event. For sunrise triggers, use 0 for automatic timing based on total zone duration.

azimuth_angle.name: Azimuth Angle (degrees)
azimuth_angle.description: Solar azimuth angle in degrees where 0=North, 90=East, 180=South, 270=West

account_for_duration.name: Account for Duration
account_for_duration.description: When enabled, irrigation will start early enough to finish at the specified time. When disabled, irrigation will start exactly at the specified time.

irrigation_start_triggers.no_triggers: No irrigation start triggers configured. The system will use the default behavior (sunrise with total zone duration). Add triggers to customize when irrigation starts.
irrigation_start_triggers.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete the trigger '{name}'?

validation.azimuth_invalid: Azimuth angle must be a valid number

help.sunrise_offset: For sunrise triggers: Use negative values to start before sunrise, positive to start after. Set to 0 to automatically start early enough to complete all zones before sunrise.
help.sunset_offset: For sunset triggers: Use negative values to start before sunset, positive to start after sunset.
help.azimuth_explanation: Solar azimuth is the compass direction of the sun. 0°=North, 90°=East, 180°=South, 270°=West. You can enter any angle value (e.g., 450° = 90°, -30° = 330°). Use this to trigger irrigation when the sun reaches a specific position.
help.multiple_triggers: You can configure multiple triggers. Each enabled trigger will independently schedule irrigation starts.

weather_skip.title: Skip Conditions
weather_skip.description: Automatically skip irrigation when conditions are unfavorable. Precipitation check requires a weather service. Temperature and wind checks also require a weather service.
weather_skip.threshold_description: Minimum total precipitation (in mm) forecast across the look-ahead window to skip irrigation.
weather_skip.lookahead_help: How many upcoming forecast days to add up when checking for rain. The forecast starts at tomorrow (today is excluded), so 1 = just the next day, 2 = the next two days, and so on.
weather_skip.wind_threshold_label: Skip if wind speed is above

coordinate_config.title: Location Coordinates
coordinate_config.description: Configure location coordinates for weather data retrieval. You can use manual coordinates different from your Home Assistant location if needed.

days_between_irrigation.title: Days Between Irrigation
days_between_irrigation.description:
Configure the minimum number of days that must pass between irrigation events. This helps control watering frequency for water conservation and plant health management.

Typical real-world use cases:
• Lawn care: 1-2 day intervals prevent overwatering
• Drought restrictions: 6+ day intervals for weekly watering
• Deep-rooted plants: 3-7 day intervals for less frequent watering
• Water conservation: Customizable based on climate and soil conditions
days_between_irrigation.label: Minimum days between irrigation
days_between_irrigation.help_text: Set to 0 to disable this feature. Values from 1-365 days are supported. This setting works alongside existing precipitation forecasting logic.

zone_sequencing.title: Zone Sequencing
zone_sequencing.description: When multiple zones need irrigation, choose whether they run at the same time or one after another. Sequential mode waits for each zone to finish before starting the next. Rotating mode cycles through zones, giving each one a limited consecutive run before moving to the next.
zone_sequencing.max_consecutive_duration_label: Max consecutive run time per zone

field_help.zone_size: The total irrigated area of this zone. Used with throughput to calculate how much water is applied per run.
field_help.zone_throughput: Total water flow of your irrigation system for this zone (litres/min in metric, gal/min in imperial). Check your sprinkler datasheet or measure by timing how long it takes to fill a known container.
field_help.zone_drainage_rate: How fast saturated soil drains excess water. ~20 mm/h suits medium/loam soil; lower (2–10) for heavy clay, higher for sandy soil.
field_help.zone_bucket: Current water deficit (negative) or surplus (positive) for this zone. Irrigation triggers when bucket drops below the threshold.
field_help.zone_maximum_bucket: Maximum moisture surplus the zone can hold. Water above this level is treated as runoff. Typical value: 50 mm.
field_help.zone_bucket_threshold: Irrigation triggers when the bucket drops below this value. Must be 0 or negative. 0 means irrigate whenever there is any deficit.
field_help.zone_multiplier: Scale factor applied to the calculated duration. Use above 1.0 to increase, below 1.0 to decrease. Useful for fine-tuning without changing physical measurements.
field_help.zone_lead_time: Extra seconds added before irrigation starts. Use for pump warm-up or system pressurisation.
field_help.zone_maximum_duration: Hard cap on any single irrigation run in seconds. Prevents runaway watering. Default: 3600 s (1 hour).
field_help.zone_linked_entity: The HA switch or valve entity controlling water flow for this zone. This entity is turned on when irrigation runs.
field_help.zone_flow_sensor: Optional sensor measuring actual water flow rate. Used for reporting only — does not affect duration calculations.
field_help.general_autoupdatedelay: Seconds to wait after HA starts before the first weather data fetch. Allows other integrations to initialise first.
field_help.general_sensor_debounce: Minimum gap in milliseconds between sensor readings to filter noise from rapidly changing sensors.
field_help.general_calctime: Time of day when irrigation durations are recalculated from collected weather data. Format: HH:MM (24-hour).
field_help.general_cleardatatime: Time of day when old weather data is purged. Must be set later than the calculation time.
field_help.general_days_between: Minimum days between irrigation events for the same zone. Set to 0 to disable (irrigate whenever deficit exists).
field_help.general_autoupdateinterval: How often weather data is collected. Choose a value that balances fresh data against API rate limits.
field_help.general_precipitation_threshold: Irrigation is skipped if total forecast precipitation across the look-ahead window exceeds this amount.
field_help.general_temp_threshold: Irrigation is skipped if the current temperature is below this value (e.g. to prevent frost damage).
field_help.general_wind_threshold: Irrigation is skipped if wind speed exceeds this value (high winds reduce efficiency and cause drift).

wizard.title: Setup Wizard
wizard.setup_complete_banner: Setup not complete. Run the wizard to get started.

welcome.title: Welcome to Smart Irrigation
welcome.intro: This wizard guides you through the four steps needed to get your first zone irrigating automatically.
welcome.step1_label: Weather Service — where to get weather data
welcome.step2_label: Calculation Module — how irrigation duration is computed
welcome.step3_label: Sensor Group — which data sources to use
welcome.tip: You can skip any step and configure it later from the Setup tab.

weather.title: Weather Service
weather.description: Choose how to get weather data. Open-Meteo is free and requires no API key — it is the easiest choice for most users.

module.title: Calculation Module
module.description: A module calculates how long to irrigate based on evapotranspiration (ET). The PyETO module (FAO-56 method) is recommended for most users.

mapping.title: Sensor Group
mapping.description: A sensor group links each weather variable to a data source. Set the key variables below — you can refine individual sensor mappings later from the Setup → Sensor Groups tab.

zone.title: First Zone
zone.description: A zone is one irrigation area (e.g. lawn, garden bed). Set the physical properties so the system can calculate the correct irrigation duration.

done.title: Setup Complete!
done.description: Your first zone is ready. Smart Irrigation will now calculate irrigation durations automatically based on weather data.
done.tip1: Go to Zones to view calculated durations and bucket values.
done.tip2: Add more zones from the Zones tab.
done.tip3: Refine all settings from the Setup tab.

confirm_close.body: Close the setup wizard? Your progress so far is saved.
